text.title: Accessibility Annotation Pattern Library Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the a11y-annotation-pattern-library methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Annotations MUST be keyed to component archetypes (button, link, dialog, menu, tabs, combobox, form field, data table). Free-form per-screen annotations are forbidden.
rule.rationale: An archetype-keyed library scales across the design system; per-screen annotations drift and rot.

rule.statement: Every interactive layer MUST carry an annotation snippet before the design is handed off. Layers without annotations fail the handoff audit.
rule.rationale: Bolted-on a11y is the dominant designer→dev failure; annotation-before-handoff blocks the regression at source.

rule.statement: Every annotation MUST resolve role + name + state. Snippets missing any of the three are rejected.
rule.rationale: WCAG 2.2 SC 4.1.2 requires the trio for assistive tech; the library codifies the minimum to prevent partial annotations.

rule.statement: Focus order MUST be specified on the design file (numbered overlay or annotation note) for every screen with >1 interactive layer.
rule.rationale: Implicit DOM order rarely matches the visual reading order; explicit focus-order annotations prevent silent regressions.

rule.statement: Every interactive component MUST carry a keyboard map (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space, arrow keys, Esc) in its annotation snippet.
rule.rationale: Custom widgets routinely ship missing arrow-key navigation; mandating the map at design time forces the decision early.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
